changes: The packaged baseline is normalized to the Moby v28.3.3 default seccomp contract without syscall reductions; the official upstream profile includes statx and the eight recorded POSIX message queue syscall names.

The original local artifact omitted statx and five real POSIX message queue syscalls (mq_getsetattr, mq_timedreceive, mq_timedreceive_time64, mq_timedsend, and mq_timedsend_time64) while including mq_send, which is a library interface name rather than the canonical Linux seccomp syscall name.

The earlier statx repair restored statx but left the message queue mismatch; this repair removes mq_send and restores the five missing syscall names as an upstream-contract normalization, producing a 281-syscall allowlist.

Human measurement on 2026-07-17 JST found that runc container init required statx with Docker Engine 29.5.3 and runc 1.3.6 for python@sha256:d764629ce0ddd8c71fd371e9901efb324a95789d2315a47db7e4d27e78f1b0e9.
A temporary profile that restored statx passed both the minimal run and the read-only, tmpfs, non-root, capability-drop, no-new-privileges sandbox boundary run in that recorded environment.

Human-shell real Docker reverification of the normalized artifact completed for F026 cases 8 through 10 with 3/3 passing and 0 failures, using Docker Engine 29.5.3, runc 1.3.6, linux/amd64, and the digest-pinned Python image python@sha256:d764629ce0ddd8c71fd371e9901efb324a95789d2315a47db7e4d27e78f1b0e9.
Candidate F030 also completed with 8/8 passing and 0 failures in the same recorded local environment.

These bounded results are limited to the recorded runtime, image, operating system, architecture, and kernel; they do not establish general compatibility or safety. Hosted workflow evidence and Human final approval remain incomplete.
